page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 4 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a budget-oriented profile with notable trade-offs. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core stress-ng performance and weak multi-core performance. However, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average at 410.01 GB/sec, though large block read and write speeds fall into the weak category. Standard processing tasks like gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and text processing achieve average tier results, while complex workloads like software compilation and ray tracing show weaker performance.

Given its shared CPU architecture and lack of local storage, this server is positioned as a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance compute. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, Redis caching, and small-scale text or image processing. It is not recommended for intensive multi-threaded applications, large-scale software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and dedicated GPUs, this instance relies on shared CPU resources, which presents a clear trade-off: it offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for general-purpose workloads but delivers lower compute performance, as evidenced by weak multi-core and single-core benchmark results. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and basic operations like Redis SET transactions perform at average levels, while intensive tasks like software compilation and large-block memory operations are weak. This server is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, caching, and development environments that do not demand sustained CPU performance.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers a cost-efficient, general-purpose profile for lightweight web serving and basic database tasks.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for budget-friendly, general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-resource virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for lightweight general-purpose workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory.
KVM hypervisor architecture with no bundled local storage or GPU.
Average performance for small-block memory caching and Redis operations.
Weak performance in sustained multi-core compute and software compilation.
Cost-efficient profile suitable for lightweight web serving and development.
